title: NCCL GitHub closed issues July GIN and CuTeDSL supplement
scope_note: Two scheduled crawler Markdown page snapshots after the June 2026 joined API corpus and after the July 1/3 page-level supplement. This is bounded page-level issue evidence only, not a full GitHub API refresh with comments, maintainer replies, review comments, confirmed root cause, fix status, release-note evidence, production postmortem, benchmark baseline, or platform compatibility rule.

joined_api_corpus.reconciliation: Issues #2246 and #1957 were not in the June joined API/comment corpus, whose curated scope ends before these July page captures.

prior_scheduled_supplement.reconciliation: The July 1/3 supplement covers #2226 and #2024 only. It already records GIN GPI/GDAKI QP-selection questions and an H20/H200 broadcast hang report, but it does not curate #2246 or #1957.

release_note_context.reconciliation: The release-note page already records GIN terminology from NCCL 2.29.7, 2.30.3, 2.30.4, and 2.30.7. These issue pages are user-reported operational questions/reports, not release-note entries.

items.title: [Question]: How does the CuTeDSL device API legally dereference the host-side `ncclDevComm` on the device?
page_visible_claims: The reporter passed a host-created `ncclDevComm` pointer by value into an H20 kernel as a raw 64-bit pointer.
The report says the first device-side field dereference, `dev_comm.lsa_rank`, failed with `cudaErrorIllegalAddress` before window access.

The reporter asks whether the design requires coherent/unified addressing such as Grace-Hopper coherence, HMM, ATS, pinned-mapped memory, managed memory, or device memory, and asks about performance overhead.
boundaries: Question/report only; no maintainer reply, root cause, supported-configuration answer, fix status, release-note guarantee, benchmark result, or general H20 compatibility rule is promoted.

items.title: [Issue]: GIN gdaki mode hardcode 4K MTU in doca modify qp
page_visible_claims: GIN GDAKI mode issue report involving the `gin_alltoall_pure` example.
The reporter says the example hung with a NIC configured for 1500 MTU.
The reporter says Wireshark observation suggested NCCL GIN used hard-coded 4K MTU behavior in `doca modify qp` / `doca modify QP` context.
The reporter asks whether this is only an implementation question or should be documented in the example README.
boundaries: Issue-body report only; no maintainer answer, DOCA/QP root cause, product fix, release-note guarantee, network compatibility rule, performance number, or production incident claim is promoted.
Promote only page-visible issue body facts from the two local raw captures.
Do not infer missing comments, maintainer replies, fix status, root cause, or supported platform requirements.
Do not update accelerator structured data, benchmark tables, source registry rows, crawler code, frontend code, dashboard code, harness code, or package files.
